project_profile.deployment_model: MindSpore is a deep learning training/inference framework library. Users install it via pip (pip install mindspore) and import it in Python code. It can also be compiled as a C++ shared library for embedding in other applications. The framework supports local execution (single machine) and distributed training across GPU/Ascend clusters.

trust_boundaries.untrusted_side: External model files (.mindir, checkpoint) provided by users or third parties
External dataset files (TFRecord, CSV, MindRecord, image files, etc.)
Other nodes in GPU cluster (no authentication for GPU clusters per SECURITY.md)
User Python code defining neural network structure (malicious code possible per SECURITY.md)
User-provided checkpoint files containing model weights

modules.name: load_mindir
modules.description: Model file loading and MindIR format parsing. Handles deserialization of protobuf-based model files.

modules.name: cluster_rpc
modules.description: Distributed training communication. TCP server/client for node-to-node message passing. GPU clusters lack authentication per SECURITY.md.

modules.name: minddata_dataset
modules.description: Dataset loading and parsing. Handles various file formats including TFRecord, CSV, COCO, MindRecord, and image/audio formats.

modules.name: minddata_mindrecord
modules.description: MindRecord format implementation. Custom data format for efficient dataset storage and retrieval.

modules.name: pybind_api
modules.description: Python-C++ binding layer. Exposes C++ functions to Python via pybind11. User Python code calls these bindings.

modules.name: backend
modules.description: Backend execution management. Handles graph execution on different hardware (Ascend GE, MS backend).

modules.name: runtime
modules.description: Runtime execution management. Memory allocation, hardware abstraction, execution pipeline.

modules.name: frontend
modules.description: Frontend compilation. JIT compilation, operator fusion, graph optimization, parallel strategy.

modules.name: core_ir
modules.description: Intermediate representation (IR). Core data structures for computational graph.

modules.name: core_proto
modules.description: Protobuf definitions for MindIR model format. Defines serialization schema for models.

modules.name: utils_checkpoint
modules.description: Checkpoint serialization format. Defines schema for saving/loading model weights.

modules.name: python_api
modules.description: Python API layer. User-facing interface for defining and training neural networks.

modules.name: ops_kernel
modules.description: Operator kernel implementations. Low-level execution of mathematical operations.

modules.name: ops_infer
modules.description: Operator inference logic. Shape and type inference for operators.

files.description: MindIR model file loading. Parses protobuf-based model files. Uses ParseFromArray/ParseFromIstream for deserialization. Multiple memcpy_s calls for tensor data copying.
MindIR protobuf schema definition. Defines model serialization format.
TCP socket operations. Contains connect(), accept(), recv(), send() calls. Network communication layer.
Collective communication operations (AllReduce, Broadcast, Send, Recv). Uses sendbuff/recvbuff buffers with Memcpy operations.
TCP server implementation. Accepts connections from distributed training nodes.
TCP socket recv/send operations. Direct recv() and sendmsg() calls.
TensorFlow TFRecord format reader. Parses TFRecord files which contain serialized TensorFlow examples.
CSV file parser. Reads and parses CSV dataset files.
COCO dataset format parser. Parses JSON annotation files and image files.
MindRecord format reader. Parses custom MindSpore dataset format.
Checkpoint protobuf schema. Defines format for model weight serialization.
Python binding initialization. Entry point for Python-C++ interface.
Python package entry point. Exposes main MindSpore APIs.
Context configuration API. Sets runtime parameters like device target, mode, etc.
Memory management. Handles tensor memory allocation and deallocation.

entry_points.justification: Users load external model files (.mindir) for inference or training. Model files may come from untrusted third parties. Per SECURITY.md: 'Model files are stored in binary mode... malicious code may be written into the model files, the code are loaded and executed.'
entry_points.description: Load MindIR model file from disk or buffer. Parses protobuf format and constructs computational graph.

entry_points.justification: Users can load model from memory buffer. Buffer contents may originate from untrusted sources (downloaded model, received from network).
entry_points.description: Load MindIR model from memory buffer (for in-memory deserialization).

entry_points.justification: Distributed training nodes connect to each other. GPU clusters lack authentication per SECURITY.md: 'If GPUs or other clusters are used for training, identity authentication and secure transmission are not provided.'
entry_points.description: TCP client connects to remote server for distributed training.

entry_points.justification: TCP server accepts connections from other nodes. GPU cluster connections lack authentication.
entry_points.description: TCP server accepts incoming connection from distributed training node.

entry_points.justification: Receives data from connected nodes via recv(). Data may include gradients, model parameters, etc. from potentially untrusted nodes.
entry_points.description: Receive data from network socket for distributed communication.

entry_points.justification: Collective operation receives data buffers from other nodes. Uses recvbuff which may contain malicious data in GPU clusters without authentication.
entry_points.description: AllReduce collective operation - receives and processes data from all nodes.

entry_points.justification: Users load TFRecord files containing training data. Files may come from untrusted sources.
Users load CSV files containing dataset. CSV files are user-provided and may contain malicious content.
Users load MindRecord format files. MindRecord is a custom binary format that may come from untrusted sources.
COCO dataset loader parses JSON annotation files and images. User-provided annotation files may contain malicious JSON.
Python binding entry point. User Python code calls C++ functions through this layer. Per SECURITY.md: 'user-defined computational graph structure... malicious code may exist.'
entry_points.description: Python-C++ binding initialization. Exposes C++ APIs to Python users.

entry_points.justification: User imports MindSpore and writes Python code to define neural networks. Malicious Python code may be executed.
entry_points.description: Python package entry point. Users write Python code that calls MindSpore APIs.

attack_surfaces: MindIR Model File Loading: load_model.cc uses protobuf ParseFromArray/ParseFromIstream to deserialize external .mindir files. Malicious model files could trigger deserialization vulnerabilities or cause memory corruption in memcpy_s operations.
Checkpoint Loading: checkpoint.proto defines serialization format for model weights. Checkpoint files may contain malicious data.
Distributed Training RPC: cluster/rpc module implements TCP communication. GPU clusters lack authentication. Nodes send/receive gradients and parameters. Malicious nodes could inject malicious data.
TFRecord Parsing: tf_reader_op.cc parses TensorFlow example format. External TFRecord files may contain malicious serialized examples.
CSV Parsing: csv_op.cc parses CSV files. CSV parsing may be vulnerable to CSV injection or malformed data.
MindRecord Parsing: mindrecord_op.cc and mindspore/ccsrc/minddata/mindrecord/ parse custom binary format. Malformed MindRecord files could trigger parsing vulnerabilities.
COCO JSON Parsing: coco_op.cc parses JSON annotation files. JSON parsing may be vulnerable to malformed JSON attacks.
Image File Loading: Various image dataset ops (image_folder_op.cc, etc.) load and decode image files. Image decoding libraries may have vulnerabilities.
Python Code Execution: User Python code defines computational graphs. Per SECURITY.md, malicious code may exist in user-defined network structures.
Memory Operations: Multiple memcpy_s and huge_memcpy calls in load_model.cc and collective_ops_impl.cc. Buffer overflow potential if size calculations are incorrect.
Protobuf Deserialization: All protobuf-based formats (mind_ir.proto, checkpoint.proto, comm.proto, etc.) rely on protobuf library. Malformed protobuf messages could trigger parsing vulnerabilities.
